title: cymbopogon laniger
intro: Cymbopogon laniger, also known as Woolly lemongrass, is a species of grass native to tropical Asia and Australia, characterized by its soft, woolly leaves and stems. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities, which are attributed to its essential oils and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Cymbopogon laniger include its potential to reduce stress, improve digestion, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia, making it a popular herbal remedy. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, essential oils, and infused water, which can be consumed or applied topically to harness its medicinal properties.
